so it commutes; naturality in the two arguments is checked separately and then combined,
  the tensor of the two component morphisms factoring as \(f^*a \otimes f^*b\) by
  bifunctoriality of \(\otimes_Y\).

  The fourth square --- the naturality of \(\mathtt{sheafifyTensorUnitIso}\)
  (\cref{lem:sheafify_tensor_unit_iso_natural}) --- is the one square that is \emph{not}
  closed by the formal monoidal interchange (whisker-exchange) law. When the comparison is
  expanded into its whisker factors, the whiskers of the sheafification unit \(\eta\) and
  the factors coming from the tensor-of-morphisms \(p \otimes q\) are framed by two
  monoidal structures on \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\) that agree definitionally but not
  syntactically; the interchange law relates morphisms only within a single such framing
  and so cannot bridge the crossing between them. The square is instead closed by
  \emph{descending to sections}. Applying the extensionality principle for
  presheaf-of-modules morphisms (\(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.Hom.ext}\)) reduces the square
  to an equality of its components over each open \(U\). Over \(U\) the whiskers unfold to
  honest tensor-of-morphisms maps and the two legs of the comparison distribute into
  concrete \(\mathtt{ModuleCat}\)-maps; in this distribution the carrier of the sheaf
  appears in two spellings --- \(\mathtt{Sheaf.val}\) and the forgetful image along
  \(\_\,\circ\,\mathtt{forget}_2\,\mathtt{CommRingCat}\,\mathtt{RingCat}\) --- which are
  definitionally but not syntactically equal, so the distribution is carried out by
  definitional-keyed rather than head-keyed rewriting. After \(\mathtt{ModuleCat}\)
  extensionality (\(\mathtt{ModuleCat.hom\_ext}\)) the goal becomes an \emph{instance-free,
  element-level} identity on the tensor product \((M \otimes_X N).\mathrm{obj}\,U\). It is
  closed by \(\mathtt{TensorProduct}\) induction: on a pure tensor \(p \otimes q\) the
  identity is exactly the sectionwise naturality of \(\eta\) in each argument separately,
  \[
    p.\mathrm{app}\,U \,\circ\, \eta_{M'}.\mathrm{app}\,U
      = \eta_{M}.\mathrm{app}\,U \,\circ\, (f^*a).\mathrm{val}.\mathrm{app}\,U,
    \qquad
    q.\mathrm{app}\,U \,\circ\, \eta_{N'}.\mathrm{app}\,U
      = \eta_{N}.\mathrm{app}\,U \,\circ\, (f^*b).\mathrm{val}.\mathrm{app}\,U,
  \]
  combined bilinearly. Thus the fourth square reduces to the bilinear, sectionwise
  naturality of the sheafification unit \(\eta\), verified one pure tensor at a time.
  % NOTE (iter-253): the whisker-exchange route described in earlier drafts is BLOCKED in
  % Lean (eta-whiskers vs. tensorHom_def-expanded factors carry two defeq-but-non-syntactic

\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}
  [Section-level naturality of the tensor/unit reconciliation
   \(\mathtt{sheafifyTensorUnitIso}\)]

\begin{lemma}

5705	  group of morphisms \(M|_W \to N|_W\) of presheaves of abelian groups, is itself a
5706	  \emph{sheaf of types} --- this is the ``hom into a sheaf is a sheaf'' principle

\begin{proof}
  \uses{lem:internal_hom_eval, lem:internal_hom_isSheaf}
  At the presheaf level the dual of the unit \(\mathbf{1} = \mathcal{O}_Y\) is the internal
  hom \(\mathcal{H}om(\mathbf{1}, \mathbf{1})\), whose value on an open \(U\) is the module
  of \(\mathcal{O}(U)\)-linear endomorphisms of \(\mathbf{1}|_U\). Each such endomorphism is
  determined by its value at the global section \(1\) and equals multiplication by that
  value; hence \emph{evaluation at \(1\)} is itself the sectionwise isomorphism
  \(\mathcal{H}om(\mathbf{1}, \mathbf{1}) \cong \mathbf{1}\) of presheaves of modules, with
  inverse the scalar-action map \(r \mapsto (\,\cdot\,r)\). (No left unitor is needed: the
  identification is the direct evaluation-at-\(1\) / global-scalar-multiplication
  isomorphism, not the composite through \(\mathbf{1} \otimes \mathbf{1} \cong \mathbf{1}\).)
  Sheafifying through the sheaf-level dual of \cref{lem:internal_hom_isSheaf} yields
  \(\mathtt{dual}\,\mathcal{O}_Y \cong \mathcal{O}_Y\).
\end{proof}
